entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: Improved varieties of groundnut used to enhance household food security and reduce poverty

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food

entities.name: POVERTY
entities.description: The state of lacking sufficient financial resources to meet basic needs

entities.name: NORTHERN NIGERIA
entities.description: The geographic region where the study on farm households was conducted

entities.name: ENDOGENOUS SWITCHING REGRESSION MODELS
entities.description: Statistical models employed to control for potential endogeneity biases in the evaluation of IGVs

entities.name: FOOD INSECURITY
entities.description: The lack of consistent access to enough food for an active, healthy life

entities.name: COUNTERFACTUAL ANALYSES
entities.description: Analyses used to estimate the potential benefits for non-adopting households had they adopted IGVs

entities.name: GROUNDNUT SEED SYSTEMS
entities.description: The infrastructure and processes for producing and distributing quality groundnut seeds to smallholder farmers

relationships.description: Adoption of improved varieties significantly increases the likelihood of household per capita consumption and food security
Improved agricultural technologies for groundnut are promoted as ways of reducing poverty
The study on groundnut variety adoption was conducted using data from farm households in Northern Nigeria
Used to evaluate the relationship between improved groundnut varieties and household food security
Adoption of improved varieties reduces the probability of households' vulnerability to food insecurity
Used to show that non-adopting households could have enjoyed benefits from improved groundnut varieties
Needed to make quality seeds readily available to smallholder farmers
